summary: When reverse proxy authentication is enabled, the application trusts the X-WEBAUTH-USER header without validating that the request actually came from the trusted reverse proxy, allowing attackers to impersonate any user including administrators.
expected_security_impact: Unauthenticated attackers can impersonate any user including administrators by setting the X-WEBAUTH-USER header, leading to complete account takeover and privilege escalation.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully demonstrated that when ENABLE_REVERSE_PROXY_AUTHENTICATION is enabled, an attacker can authenticate as any user (including admin) by simply setting the X-WEBAUTH-USER header in the HTTP request.
verification_method: The exploit script enables reverse proxy authentication, then sends requests with spoofed X-WEBAUTH-USER headers to access admin-only endpoints and verify the impersonation works.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP 200 response when accessing admin API with spoofed X-WEBAUTH-USER header
No password or credentials required - header alone grants authentication
User object in response shows the spoofed username
Admin privileges granted when spoofing admin username

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the Gogs repository from GitHub and checks out the vulnerable version
